questions.question: 2 (a) find an equation of the tangent to C at P, giving your answer in the form y = mx + c , where m and c are constants, (3) (b) find the value of k, (2) (c) find f (x) , writing the answer in simplest form. (5) _____________________________________________________________________________________ _____________________________________________________________________________________ ______

Correct shape and position B1 Correct equation of vertical asymptote. E.g. x = ‚ąí6 oe e.g. x + 6 = 0 B1 Correct y intercept 0, ÔÉ¶ÔÉ∂ ÔÉßÔÉ∑ ÔÉ®ÔÉł B1 (3) Notes There must be a sketch to score marks in (a) Allow the equation of the asymptote and the y intersection to be identified away from the sketch but they must clearly be identified as the asymptote and/or the intersection and correspond to their sketch. If there is any ambiguity, the sketch takes precedence. (a) B1: Correct shape and position. The right hand branch should ‚ÄĘ be in quadrants 1 and 2 only (this includes just touching the y-axis) ‚ÄĘ approach the x-axis from above on the right ‚ÄĘ approach the vertical upwards on the left in quadrant 2 The left hand branch should ‚ÄĘ be in quadrant 3 only ‚ÄĘ approach the x-axis from below on the left ‚ÄĘ approach the vertical downwards on the right The branches must not clearly overlap. The curve must not clearly turn back on itself at the vertical or horizontal asymptotes but condone ‚Äúslips of the pen‚ÄĚ. B1: Correct equation for vertical asymptote. The curve must be asymptotic here for their curve and for both branches if drawn. The asymptote does not need to be ‚Äúdrawn‚ÄĚ e.g. dotted or solid line. Must be an equation (not just ‚ąí6 marked on the x-axis) B1: Correct y intercept. The curve must only cross the y-axis at this point. Allow as marked on the y-axis or as 0, ÔÉ¶ÔÉ∂ ÔÉßÔÉ∑ ÔÉ®ÔÉł and condone ,0 ÔÉ¶ÔÉ∂ ÔÉßÔÉ∑ ÔÉ®ÔÉł as long as it is in the correct position on the positive y-axis. Also allow y = if it is clearly indicating the point of intersection and not the equation of an asymptote.
